test_description: This test case evaluates the system's ability to retrieve and synthesize information from multiple, separate document sections to answer a complex question.
question: [Judge] If the power dissipation value Pdiss of boost converter circuitry exceeds a threshold, the controller will reduce the input current limit to prevent excessive die temperature increases; however, in gate-all-around nanosheet transistors, the inner spacer thickness (IS thickness) does not affect the gate stack TDDB reliability. (True/False)

gold_context_sources.evidence: If the determined power dissipation value exceeds a threshold power dissipation value, the controller reduces the input current limit to reduce the risk that the on-chip power dissipation could cause excessive heating of the semiconductor die on which the boost converter circuitry is implemented.

The understanding of IS thickness (THK) caused performance and reliability trade-off is very critical for NS transistors. Moreover, limited study on gate stack TDDB impact from sheet geometry has been published.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is False. Reasoning: The controller reduces the input current limit when the power dissipation value Pdiss exceeds a threshold to prevent excessive die temperature increases, as described in the boost converter circuitry control system. However, in gate-all-around nanosheet transistors, the inner spacer thickness (IS thickness) critically affects both performance and reliability trade-offs, including gate stack TDDB reliability. Therefore, the statement that IS thickness does not affect gate stack TDDB reliability is incorrect.
